title: catalpa × hybrida var. purpurea
intro: Catalpa × hybrida var. purpurea, also known as the Purple Catalpa or Northern Catalpa, is a deciduous tree hybrid that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which are known to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The health benefits of Catalpa × hybrida var. purpurea include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and prevent cardiovascular disease. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water, and are often used to create teas and remedies for various health concerns.
